messages.content: You are given a single replicate event history from a Moran birth-death process. Mutants (type A) may have different fitness from non-mutants (type B). Your task is to decide whether the mutant fixation probability is above 0.5 or below 0.5. Output X for above 0.5 and O for below 0.5. Use the trace only. Reason from two competing signals: (a) apparent reproductive advantage or disadvantage, and (b) numerical advantage or disadvantage, especially the starting mutant count and whether mutants stay near fixation or near extinction. Do not decide from fitness signal alone. In particular, when mutants already occupy almost the whole population, they may still have fixation probability above 0.5 even if they seem somewhat less fit. When mutants are very rare, they may still have fixation probability below 0.5 even if they seem somewhat fitter. Return valid JSON with exactly one key: label, equal to X or O. Do not output any other text.

You are given a single replicate event history from a Moran birth-death process. Mutants (type A) may have different fitness from non-mutants (type B). Reason from two competing signals: (a) apparent reproductive advantage or disadvantage, and (b) numerical advantage or disadvantage, especially the starting mutant count and whether mutants stay near fixation or near extinction. Do not decide from fitness signal alone. In particular, when mutants already occupy almost the whole population, they may still have fixation probability above 0.5 even if they seem somewhat less fit. When mutants are very rare, they may still have fixation probability below 0.5 even if they seem somewhat fitter. Return valid JSON with exactly one key: label, equal to X or O. Do not output any other text.

Trace file: exp001_run02.csv
Below is the observable Moran-process event history in CSV format. Classify whether the fixation probability rho is greater than 0.5 (X) or less than 0.5 (O).

step,event,birth_index,birth_type,death_index,death_type,mutants_before,mutants_after,N
0,13A:9A,13,A,9,A,14,14,15
1,0A:10A,0,A,10,A,14,14,15
2,10A:6A,10,A,6,A,14,14,15
3,6A:4A,6,A,4,A,14,14,15
4,10A:7A,10,A,7,A,14,14,15
5,13A:2A,13,A,2,A,14,14,15
6,11A:3A,11,A,3,A,14,14,15
7,2A:13A,2,A,13,A,14,14,15
8,5A:14B,5,A,14,B,14,15,15
